LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MAY-19-2023 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH TRACKS THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE EXCEPTION THAT IT SHOULD NOW STATE LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED AND SHOULD STATE A DATE BY WHICH AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TO BE FILED BY. THE PROPOSED ORDER SHOULD BE IN A WORD EDITABLE FORMAT AND SHOULD BE EMAILED TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG BY THE END OF TODAY. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. FOR PLAINTIFFS SHELLEY MARTIN-GOULARTE AND JEFFERY GOULARTE -- JUDGE ANNE CHRISTINE MASSULLO INDICATED TO THE PARTIES THAT SHE IS RECUSING HERSELF FROM HEARING THE MOTION - DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/23/24 IS CONTINUED TO 10/28/24 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. DISCOVERY TO TRACK THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/23/24. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION WHICH IS IN VIOLATION OF SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. THE COURT ORDERS MOVING PARTY TO PAY $60.00 FILING FEE VIA E-FILING BY END OF DAY TODAY WHICH IS THE COST FOR FILING PAPERS THAT MOVING PARTY WOULD HAVE TO FILE AGAIN WITH A COURTESY COPY IN ORDER TO GET THE NEW TRIAL DATE. MINI MINUTES:

WILLIAM T. BANDETTINI, ESQ., THE VEEN FIRM LLP (415) 673-4800 AL.Team@VeenFirm.com
ATTORNEYS FOR Plaintiffs SHELLY MARTIN-GOULARTE AND JEFFREY GOULARTE

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows::

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Jeffrey Goulartes Deposition Testimony is DENIED, but without prejudice to its being renewed after a genuine meet and confer effort is made within 20 days of the filing date of this order. Any such renewed motion must, obviously, satisfy the moving party's obligation under CCP section 2016.040. Any such renewed motion may address the witness' response to questions about his wife's 2019 injuries, but not regarding his own injuries in that same accident. [SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL AND COMPLETE DETAILS]

Counsel to plaintiff is to submit a proposed order that is verbatim to the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.132 by email to Judge Pro Tem Philip Ward at psw@hassard.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Phillip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
